summary:
## Summary

Built the customer-facing fault-report submission screen on top of the existing `fault-data-foundation` (types/store/actions in `app/lib/faults/`).

**Changes:**
- `app/lib/faults/actions.ts` — added `submitFaultReport` server action: validates untrusted `FormData` (all fields required except `safety_advice`), returns per-field errors on failure, or calls the existing `createFault` to write into the shared in-memory store on success.
- `app/fault-report-form.tsx` (new) — client component using `useActionState` + `useFormStatus`, per the Next.js 16 forms guide (`node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/forms.md`). Renders the form (customer name, brand/model, description, urgency select, optional safety advice, submit button) or, on success, a confirmation panel with the fault's reference ID and urgency, plus a "Report another fault" link.
- `app/page.tsx` — replaced the scaffold template with the fault-report screen, rendering `FaultReportForm`.

**Verification:**
- `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass cleanly.
- Directly exercised `submitFaultReport`: an empty submission correctly returned all five field errors; a valid submission wrote a new record into the shared `store.ts` map (`store size 1`), confirming the customer form and manager-side store are wired together correctly.
